title: JsonUI binding resolution semantics (canonical)
notes: Hand-authored canonical source (renderer SSoT track 15, declared 2026-07-24). This file IS the SSoT for how '@{...}' expressions resolve; runtimes and code generators conform to it, not the other way around. Companion test vectors: shared/core/binding_vectors.json. Canonical choices are design decisions, not majority votes of existing implementations.

syntax.inner: path [ '??' default ] | '!' path (boolean value context only)
syntax.path: identifier segments joined by '.', with optional bracket index: profile.name, items[0].title
syntax.whitespace: Whitespace around 'inner', around '??', and around the default literal is insignificant.

text.description: Mixed-text interpolation. A string attribute may contain any number of '@{...}' occurrences with surrounding literal text; each occurrence is replaced by the stringified resolved value.

value.description: Whole-value binding for typed attributes (number / bool / color / enum / src / visibility / ...). The whole attribute value must be exactly one '@{...}' expression; no surrounding text.

embedParams.description: Embed 'params' leaf values. Leaves are scalar literals or one whole '@{...}' expression resolved against the PARENT screen's data. Intermediate nodes are literal objects; arrays are unsupported (validator error, established by the embed isolated+params track).

nameRef.description: Event / handler name references (onclick etc.). '@{name}' and the bare name are equivalent spellings of a handler NAME; this is name extraction, not value resolution. Path, default, and negation are not applicable.

twoWay.description: Two-way bindings (TextField text, Switch isOn, ...). The expression must be a single flat identifier: no dots, no brackets, no '??', no '!'. This keeps write-back and change-handler wiring trivially derivable on every platform.

pathResolution.rule: Look up the raw inner string as a flat key in the data map first; if absent, split on '.' and traverse. A data map that literally contains the key 'a.b' therefore shadows the nested path a.b (determinism guarantee).

arrayIndex.syntax: name[index] with a non-negative integer literal
arrayIndex.rule: Canonical array access is bracket syntax (items[0].title). Out-of-range or non-array => the path is unresolved (never a crash).
arrayIndex.nonCanonical: Dot-numeric segments (items.0.title) are a frozen UIKit-runtime legacy; not canonical, not to be added elsewhere.

pathResolution.intermediateMiss: A missing or non-object intermediate node makes the whole path unresolved; it must never crash or throw (web: generated user data paths use optional chaining).

defaultOperator.arity: Exactly one '??' per expression. More than one is a validator error; runtimes split on the FIRST '??' only (the remainder is part of the default literal parse and fails closed to unresolved).

defaultLiteral.string: Quoted with double OR single quotes: "x" or 'x' (single quotes are canonical-new; historical KJUI accepted only double quotes).
defaultLiteral.null: null => treated as unresolved (falls through to the context's unresolved behavior)

defaultOperator.rule: If the path resolves (including to false, 0, or empty string), the resolved value wins. Only an UNRESOLVED path yields the default. In 'value' context the default is typed (true stays a bool, 42 stays a number).

unresolved.text: Empty string per occurrence (plus a debug-level log listing available keys).
unresolved.value: The attribute's default value applies (component-defined). Never a crash, never a rendered error.
unresolved.embedParams: The key is OMITTED from the resolved params object so the embedded screen's own data-section defaultValue applies.
unresolved.twoWay: The attribute's default; write-back targets the declared identifier regardless.

fallbackPrecedence: 1. data-section defaultValue (merged into the data map before any binding resolves)
2. inline '??' default (only reached when the key is absent after step 1)

negation.rule: Resolve the path, coerce to bool (see coercion), then invert. An unresolved path stays unresolved (attribute default applies WITHOUT inversion). Any other context is a validator error; runtimes treat the token as an ordinary (unresolvable) key and fall through to the context's unresolved behavior.

accepts: any scalar via text stringification rules
string.otherwise: unresolved (objects/arrays do not stringify into value context)

textStringification.number: JSON-faithful: integral numbers render with no decimal point ('5', not '5.0'); non-integral render with their decimal digits.
textStringification.objectOrArray: unresolved (empty string) — never a platform debug dump

embedParamsTypes: Resolved leaves keep native JSON types (bool stays bool, 5 stays integer 5). Unresolved leaves are dropped (see unresolved.embedParams).

collectionCellScope.guaranteed: The item object's own fields, flat, plus the reserved key 'index' (0-based).
collectionCellScope.parentData: NOT guaranteed. Depending on parent-screen keys inside a cell is non-portable (validator warning).
collectionCellScope.itemPrefix: '@{item.xxx}' is NOT canonical; scaffolds must emit flat field names.

validatorRules.rule: More than one '??' in a single binding expression.
Two-way binding expression is not a single flat identifier (contains '.', '[', '??', or '!').
'!' negation used outside a boolean value context.
'??' default used in an embed params leaf (defaults belong to the embedded screen's data section).
Array anywhere inside params (pre-existing rule from the embed isolated+params track; restated here).
Cell layout binds a key that is only present in the parent screen's data.
